reproducibility_signals: Named tools (Express, Redis, JWT validator, Sentry, Postgres-backed sessions, gRPC token issuance, Datadog dashboards, OAuth) are all real components used in real auth-service architectures.

The 'three independent events converge' pattern (incident + leadership change + headcount freeing) is observable in real engineering organisations and well-documented in retrospectives of major rewrites.
The behaviour described — senior engineer drafting an imposter-recoil Slack message before a long-awaited project — is a recurring real pattern.
The implication is testable as a roadmap-conversation check: was the convergence engineered or unsought?
verdict_reason: The analog reproduces in code (the auth-service rewrite is realistic), in organisational practice (the convergence pattern), and in observable behaviour. Not pure metaphor. PASS.
